speaker: CELIA
text: Why, cousin! Why, Rosalind! Cupid have mercy,

speaker: CELIA
text: not a word?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Not one to throw at a dog.

speaker: CELIA
text: No, thy words are too precious to be cast away

speaker: CELIA
text: upon curs. Throw some of them at me. Come, lame

speaker: CELIA
text: me with reasons.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Then there were two cousins laid up, when

speaker: ROSALIND
text: the one should be lamed with reasons, and the

speaker: ROSALIND
text: other mad without any.

speaker: CELIA
text: But is all this for your father?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: No, some of it is for my child’s father. O,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: how full of briers is this working-day world!

speaker: CELIA
text: They are but burs, cousin, thrown upon thee in

speaker: CELIA
text: holiday foolery. If we walk not in the trodden paths,

speaker: CELIA
text: our very petticoats will catch them.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I could shake them off my coat. These burs

speaker: ROSALIND
text: are in my heart.

speaker: CELIA
text: Hem them away.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I would try, if I could cry “hem” and have

speaker: ROSALIND
text: him.

speaker: CELIA
text: Come, come, wrestle with thy affections.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: O, they take the part of a better wrestler

speaker: ROSALIND
text: than myself.

speaker: CELIA
text: O, a good wish upon you. You will try in time, in

speaker: CELIA
text: despite of a fall. But turning these jests out of

speaker: CELIA
text: service, let us talk in good earnest. Is it possible on

speaker: CELIA
text: such a sudden you should fall into so strong a liking

speaker: CELIA
text: with old Sir Rowland’s youngest son?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: The Duke my father loved his father dearly.

speaker: CELIA
text: Doth it therefore ensue that you should love his

speaker: CELIA
text: son dearly? By this kind of chase I should hate him,

speaker: CELIA
text: for my father hated his father dearly. Yet I hate not

speaker: CELIA
text: Orlando.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: No, faith, hate him not, for my sake.

speaker: CELIA
text: Why should I not? Doth he not deserve well?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Let me love him for that, and do you love

speaker: ROSALIND
text: him because I do.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Look, here comes the Duke.

speaker: CELIA
text: With his eyes full of anger.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: Mistress, dispatch you with your safest haste,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: And get you from our court.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Me, uncle?

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: You, cousin.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: Within these ten days if that thou beest found

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: So near our public court as twenty miles,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: Thou diest for it.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I do beseech your Grace,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Let me the knowledge of my fault bear with me.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: If with myself I hold intelligence

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Or have acquaintance with mine own desires,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: If that I do not dream or be not frantic—

speaker: ROSALIND
text: As I do trust I am not—then, dear uncle,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Never so much as in a thought unborn

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Did I offend your Highness.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: Thus do all traitors.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: If their purgation did consist in words,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: They are as innocent as grace itself.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: Let it suffice thee that I trust thee not.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Yet your mistrust cannot make me a traitor.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Tell me whereon the likelihood depends.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: Thou art thy father’s daughter. There’s enough.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: So was I when your Highness took his dukedom.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: So was I when your Highness banished him.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Treason is not inherited, my lord,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Or if we did derive it from our friends,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: What’s that to me? My father was no traitor.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Then, good my liege, mistake me not so much

speaker: ROSALIND
text: To think my poverty is treacherous.

speaker: CELIA
text: Dear sovereign, hear me speak.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: Ay, Celia, we stayed her for your sake;

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: Else had she with her father ranged along.

speaker: CELIA
text: I did not then entreat to have her stay.

speaker: CELIA
text: It was your pleasure and your own remorse.

speaker: CELIA
text: I was too young that time to value her,

speaker: CELIA
text: But now I know her. If she be a traitor,

speaker: CELIA
text: Why, so am I. We still have slept together,

speaker: CELIA
text: Rose at an instant, learned, played, eat together,

speaker: CELIA
text: And, wheresoe’er we went, like Juno’s swans

speaker: CELIA
text: Still we went coupled and inseparable.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: She is too subtle for thee, and her smoothness,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: Her very silence, and her patience

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: Speak to the people, and they pity her.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: Thou art a fool. She robs thee of thy name,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: And thou wilt show more bright and seem more virtuous

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: When she is gone. Then open not thy lips.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: Firm and irrevocable is my doom

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: Which I have passed upon her. She is banished.

speaker: CELIA
text: Pronounce that sentence then on me, my liege.

speaker: CELIA
text: I cannot live out of her company.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: You are a fool.—You, niece, provide yourself.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: If you outstay the time, upon mine honor

speaker:
DUKE
               
              FREDERICK
text: And in the greatness of my word, you die.

speaker: CELIA
text: O my poor Rosalind, whither wilt thou go?

speaker: CELIA
text: Wilt thou change fathers? I will give thee mine.

speaker: CELIA
text: I charge thee, be not thou more grieved than I am.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I have more cause.

speaker: CELIA
text: Thou hast not, cousin.

speaker: CELIA
text: Prithee, be cheerful. Know’st thou not the Duke

speaker: CELIA
text: Hath banished me, his daughter?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: That he hath not.

speaker: CELIA
text: No, hath not? Rosalind lacks then the love

speaker: CELIA
text: Which teacheth thee that thou and I am one.

speaker: CELIA
text: Shall we be sundered? Shall we part, sweet girl?

speaker: CELIA
text: No, let my father seek another heir.

speaker: CELIA
text: Therefore devise with me how we may fly,

speaker: CELIA
text: Whither to go, and what to bear with us,

speaker: CELIA
text: And do not seek to take your change upon you,

speaker: CELIA
text: To bear your griefs yourself and leave me out.

speaker: CELIA
text: For, by this heaven, now at our sorrows pale,

speaker: CELIA
text: Say what thou canst, I’ll go along with thee.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Why, whither shall we go?

speaker: CELIA
text: To seek my uncle in the Forest of Arden.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Alas, what danger will it be to us,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Maids as we are, to travel forth so far?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Beauty provoketh thieves sooner than gold.

speaker: CELIA
text: I’ll put myself in poor and mean attire,

speaker: CELIA
text: And with a kind of umber smirch my face.

speaker: CELIA
text: The like do you. So shall we pass along

speaker: CELIA
text: And never stir assailants.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Were it not better,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Because that I am more than common tall,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: That I did suit me all points like a man?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: A gallant curtal-ax upon my thigh,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: A boar-spear in my hand, and in my heart

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Lie there what hidden woman’s fear there will,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: We’ll have a swashing and a martial outside—

speaker: ROSALIND
text: As many other mannish cowards have

speaker: ROSALIND
text: That do outface it with their semblances.

speaker: CELIA
text: What shall I call thee when thou art a man?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I’ll have no worse a name than Jove’s own page,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: And therefore look you call me Ganymede.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: But what will you be called?

speaker: CELIA
text: Something that hath a reference to my state:

speaker: CELIA
text: No longer Celia, but Aliena.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: But, cousin, what if we assayed to steal

speaker: ROSALIND
text: The clownish fool out of your father’s court?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Would he not be a comfort to our travel?

speaker: CELIA
text: He’ll go along o’er the wide world with me.

speaker: CELIA
text: Leave me alone to woo him. Let’s away

speaker: CELIA
text: And get our jewels and our wealth together,

speaker: CELIA
text: Devise the fittest time and safest way

speaker: CELIA
text: To hide us from pursuit that will be made

speaker: CELIA
text: After my flight. Now go we in content

speaker: CELIA
text: To liberty, and not to banishment.
